notes: Caucasian healthy volunteers, single 100 mg PO. SA group: *5A/*5B, *5A/*6A, *5B/*5B, *5B/*6A, *6A/*6A. RA group: *4/*4. AUC/dose values: SA 0.091 (range 0.067-0.132) h/L, RA 0.031 (range 0.027-0.037) h/L. SDs estimated from ranges via range/4 approximation. N(RA)=3 marginally violates protocol §1.5 #5 (N≥4 per group) — same caveat shape as SLCO1B1 Niemi 2006 men-stratum N(CC)=3. CI on SA/RA ratio approximated via delta-method from log-scale variance. Half-life surprisingly similar across groups (3.58 SA vs 3.82 RA) — suggests intra-group variability dominates the small-N rapid arm.

column29: Aggregated across 41 studies, NOT a primary single-study extraction. AUC0-24 values; SDs reflect inter-study heterogeneity not intra-study. Aggregate cannot enter DerSimonian-Laird pool because Ns per study not separately reported in accessible abstract. Retained as a sanity-check on the central tendency: aggregate SA/RA ≈ 2.07 (lower than Kinzig-Schippers 2.94 but within plausible range; difference likely reflects (a) the 13/3 Kinzig sample's skew toward more-extreme SA haplotypes, vs aggregated cohorts with mixed *5A/*6A/*7A etc.; (b) inclusion of TB patient cohorts in the aggregate, which differ from healthy volunteers).

notes: 33 healthy Asians, popPK analysis. CL estimates: fast 65 L/h, intermediate 35 L/h, slow 8 L/h. SA/RA AUC ratio implied via inverse-CL = 65/8 = 8.13. NOT a per-group means + SDs extraction — this is the popPK MODEL's typical-value separation, which can over-estimate vs direct observation. Excluded from primary pool per protocol §1.7 sensitivity #4 (popPK ≠ direct-observation). Retained as supplementary signal that Asian-cohort SA/RA effect may be larger than Kinzig-Schippers Caucasians.

journal: Am J Respir Crit Care Med
notes: 60 TB patients (47 with NAT2 genotyping). Demonstrated trimodality. Per-phenotype AUC means + SDs NOT in accessible abstract. Excluded from primary pool — supplementary citation that NAT2 trimodality is real. Full text would need separate access.

extraction_confidence: TB patients, 4 studies pooled, doses 3/5/6/10-12 mg/kg. Reports EBA(90) at AUC0-inf = 10.52 µg·h/mL but does NOT expose per-phenotype AUC means in abstract. N by phenotype reported (slow N varies by dose arm). Excluded from primary AUC pool — useful for clinical-relevance context but not for direct meta-analysis without full-text extraction.

Dose-escalation study INCLUDED ONLY RA-type (N=8) and IA-type (N=8) — NO slow acetylators. Cannot extract SA/RA ratio. Excluded from primary pool per protocol §1.3 #4 (requires SA group reported). Retained for completeness of search log.
